title: Fix Recruitment Workflow
body:
### Description
Fixing the existing recruitment workflow.

### Solution and Design
Fixing the existing recruitment workflow.

### Type of change
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [x] Breaking change
- [x] Docs update

### Checklist
- [x] Atomic
- [x] Conforms to guidelines
- [x] Documented
- [x] Added required tests

step_plan.summary: Repair the seeded Recruitment Workflow so resume iteration, job-description reading, deterministic YES/NO branching, and termination behave reliably; email is sent only for YES matches and the workflow ends cleanly when the queue is exhausted.

steps.operation: Fix recruitment workflow step creation and prompts to ensure deterministic branching, correct job-description evaluation, robust task-queue termination, and email-only-on-YES behavior (including a small smoke validation of created edges/prompts before commit).
steps.reason: The recruitment workflow is fully defined here, and current step4 creation appears to misuse positional arguments (likely mis-assigning instruction/completion_prompt), while step2/step4 prompts do not strongly enforce machine-parseable outputs (TASK_QUEUE JSON, YES/NO token) needed for correct routing via conditional edges.
steps.side_effects: Changes seeded DB records for the Recruitment Workflow (existing deployments may need reseeding/migration of step prompts). Conditional routing becomes stricter (breaking change for any runtime that relied on verbose answers). May require updating any docs/examples that reference the previous prompts; add/adjust a seed-time assertion/smoke check so failures surface early.
